name: Base Attack
description: Your default combat action, available to everyone. It can be used to apply Damage or a Basic Condition.

name: Dodge Action
description: Add your Tier to your Avoidance score until the start of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Brace Action
description: Add your Tier to your Durability score until the start of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Fortify Action
description: Add your Tier to all of your Resistances (Resolve, Stability, Vitality) until the start of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Aim Action
description: Add your Tier to your next Accuracy Check before the end of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Empower Action
description: Add your Tier to your next Damage Roll before the end of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Refine Action
description: Add your Tier to your next Condition Check before the end of your next turn. This action does not stack with itself.

name: Assist Action
description: An adjacent ally can add your Tier bonus to their next Accuracy Check, Damage Roll, or Condition Check before the end of their next turn.

name: Carry Action
description: Pick up and move willing characters or objects with you during your movement. If the target's total weight is greater than your Capacity score, you must succeed a Power check and can only move at half speed.

name: Protect Action
description: As a reaction, you can make the next non-direct attack that would hit an adjacent ally hit you instead.

name: Use Action
description: Interact with complex environmental objects like cars, computers, or heavy machinery.

name: Hasten Action
description: You can move up to your full Movement Speed again.

name: Hide Action
description: Conceal your presence with a Stealth Check vs. a searcher's Perception while outside their direct perception. Attacking while hidden grants bonuses.

name: Prepare Action
description: Delay your action until a specified trigger occurs. The GM determines if the specified scenario is specific enough.
